data_quality: v0.1 best-effort seed populated from cited public sources. Each entity's compatibility claims trace to source_ids on its attributes. Verification pass required before production launch; this dataset is sized to prove the configurator engine end-to-end, not for general consumer trust.

sources.name: Carbide 3D / Shapeoko product pages and docs
sources.scope: Shapeoko 4 Standard / XL / Pro machine specs, work envelope, spindle mount diameter, feed rates, controller details.

sources.name: Onefinity CNC product pages
sources.scope: Woodworker and Journeyman machine specs, work envelope, ballscrew details, spindle mount, controller.

sources.name: Avid CNC / Pro 4x4 product pages
sources.scope: PRO4824 and 4x4 machine specs, linear rails, drive system, work envelope, spindle mount options.

sources.name: CNCzone.com public forums
sources.scope: Community-sourced real-world feed rates, bit performance reports, compatibility caveats for spindles and fixtures, collet size gotchas.

sources.name: V1 Engineering and OpenBuilds community docs
sources.scope: DIY CNC build guidance, controller recommendations, Buildbotics and GRBL controller specs, fixture approaches for spoilboards.

sources.name: Harvey Tool and Destiny Tool public catalog (bits)
sources.scope: Bit geometry definitions, O-flute aluminum endmill specs, compression bit geometry, micro endmill diameter ranges, carbide grades for fiberglass.

sources.name: Manufacturer spec sheets (Makita, DeWalt, Mafell, VFD spindle vendors)
sources.scope: Router HP, RPM ranges, collet sizes, base diameter, cooling type for VFD spindles. Vendors: Makita, DeWalt, Mafell, generic 65 mm and 80 mm VFD spindles.

sources.name: Genmitsu / Sainsmart product pages and Inventables X-Carve Pro page
sources.scope: PROVerXL 4030 and X-Carve Pro machine specs, work envelope, spindle mount, included controller, feed rate ratings.

attributes.note: Entry-level desktop CNC; belt-drive X/Y; includes BitSetter and BitRunner relay. Ships with Makita RT0701C router mount (65 mm).
Extended X-axis; same Z and Y travel as Standard. Suitable for 32-inch signs. 65 mm spindle mount.
Full 4x4 envelope; same 65 mm router mount. Popular for tiled sign work and medium cabinet parts.
Integrated 300W spindle, automatic tool changer (ATC) with 4-tool carousel. Enclosed; quieter than open routers. Not a 65 mm router mount machine.
Ballscrew X/Y/Z; 80 mm spindle mount — fits 80 mm VFD spindles directly. High-rigidity for its price class.
Extended X (50-inch rail); same 80 mm mount. Targets door panels, long cabinet rails.
Semi-pro rack-and-pinion gantry. Controller sold separately (Masso G3 or Avid AVID-CNC board). 80 mm spindle mount; designed for 2.2 kW VFD spindle.
Budget ballscrew desktop CNC. Integrated 300 W spindle. Good for wood signs and soft plastics. 52 mm spindle mount limits router upgrades.
Subscription CAM (Easel Pro) baked in. Large belt-drive machine. 65 mm router mount; ships with DeWalt DWP611.
Plywood frame; entry-level hobbyist. Ships with DeWalt DWP611 or Makita RT0701C (65 mm). Slowest max feed in class.
Most common router for Shapeoko / BobsCNC 65 mm mounts. Variable speed dial. 1/4 inch collet only; no 1/8 inch collet without adapter.
Ships with X-Carve Pro and BobsCNC E4. Variable speed. 65 mm base; 1/4 inch collet only. LED ring useful for visibility.
German-made; CNC-optimized motor winding (low runout). Wider RPM range than Makita. 65 mm base; direct drop-in for Shapeoko / Onefinity 65 mm mounts.

entities.name: Generic 1.5 kW 65 mm Air-Cooled VFD Spindle

attributes.note: VFD-driven; quieter than trim routers under load. Requires separate VFD drive and 220 V or 110 V wiring. ER11 collet spans 1/16–1/4 inch shanks. 65 mm body fits Shapeoko / BobsCNC mounts.

entities.name: Generic 2.2 kW 80 mm Water-Cooled VFD Spindle

attributes.note: Standard upgrade for Onefinity / Avid 80 mm mounts. Needs water pump + radiator. ER20 collet accepts 1/4, 3/8, 1/2 inch shanks. Near-silent in operation.
High-end German air-cooled CNC spindle; no external water system needed. 80 mm body. ER16 collet; lower max shank than ER20 water-cooled but no plumbing required.
Downcut shear keeps top face clean; chips push into material. Best for shallow pockets and sign faces where tearout matters.
Upcut pulls chips out of slot; prevents recutting. Standard for through-cuts and deep pockets. Bottom face may show tearout on veneered plywood.
Upcut lower flutes + downcut upper flutes; clean top and bottom face simultaneously. Ideal for cabinet sheet goods.
Standard small-detail bit. Lower feed rates than 1/4 inch. 1/8 inch shank; requires 1/8 inch collet or ER11/ER16 reducer.
Micro endmill for fine detail; 1/8 inch shank (necked down). Fragile — requires rigid machine and low feed rates. Not for belt-drive machines at high speeds.
Narrow 60° angle; sharper letterforms, tighter serifs. V-carving and inlay clearance.
Wide 90° angle; bold shallow V-carve, chamfers, and inlay pockets. Most common V-bit for sign work.
3D contour finishing; scallop height depends on stepover. Typical 3D finishing: 10–15% stepover.
Fine 3D detail finishing pass. 1/8 inch shank; needs ER11 or collet adapter.

entities.name: Single-Flute O-Flute Endmill for Aluminum (solid carbide)

attributes.note: Single O-flute maximizes chip clearance in aluminum and acrylic. Run with lubricant (WD-40 mist for Al). Low chip recutting risk.
Knurl/diamond pattern on cutting edge reduces delamination in abrasive composite materials. Wear dust mask; use dust collection.
Passive drag knife held in 1/4 inch collet. No spindle rotation needed. Cuts sheet material by dragging a swiveling blade. CNC must support tangential knife offsets in CAM.
Ultra-low profile; tool path can pass over clamp body. Wedge-action grips workpiece edge. Compatible with 3/4 inch T-track.
Router table-style T-track routed or embedded in spoilboard. T-bolts + toggle clamps or step clamps slide freely. Most flexible workholding for varied stock sizes.
Sacrificial waste board surfaced flat with CNC's own spindle. Provides consistent Z reference and protects machine bed. Must be resurfaced periodically.

attributes.vacuum_source: shop vac or dedicated vacuum pump
attributes.note: Grid of vacuum zones milled into MDF or HDPE plate; sealed with gasket tape. Best for thin sheet stock. Requires airtight perimeter.
20 mm dog holes drilled in spoilboard via Parf Guide jig. Bench dogs + F-clamps for quick registration. Useful for repeat production runs.
L-shaped stop blocks fixed to T-track or bed. Repeatable XY zero for production runs without re-probing. Works with T-track kits.
Magnetic brush skirt for easy bit changes. Designed for Shapeoko (65 mm router). Mounts to Z carriage. Works with Makita RT0701C and DeWalt DWP611.
Simple slip-over brush collar for 65 mm routers. Connects to standard 1.5 inch shop vac hose. Low-cost entry option; less effective at high spindle speeds.
Rigid body with adjustable brush height. Fits 65 mm router collar. Compatible with Shapeoko and X-Carve 65 mm mounts.
Wide category; many open-source STL designs for 3D printing. Connects to 38 mm shop vac hose. Effectiveness varies by print quality and brush type.
Linux-based (Raspberry Pi). Web UI + touchscreen optional. Used in Onefinity machines. Not GRBL — uses own G-code dialect; most CAM software targets it fine. Supports 4-axis (A-axis rotary).
Standalone industrial-grade controller. No PC needed; runs from internal flash. Supports spindle VFD control via RS485/Modbus, probing, tool length sensor. Preferred for Avid PRO4824 semi-pro builds.

attributes.frame: steel rail, modular, extended X and Y
attributes.note: Largest Onefinity model; ~4x4 foot cutting envelope. Ballscrew X/Y/Z; 80 mm spindle mount for 2.2 kW VFD spindle. Modular steel rail design.
Full-sheet and double-sheet production machine. Rack-and-pinion drive; 80 mm spindle mount for industrial VFD spindle. Controller sold separately.
Classic hobbyist machine; belt-drive; 65 mm router mount ships with DeWalt DWP611. Easel CAM ecosystem tightly integrated.

attributes.frame: laser-cut birch plywood, upgraded linear motion
attributes.note: Revised E4 successor with improved linear carriage. Plywood frame; entry-level hobbyist. 65 mm router mount. Ships with DeWalt DWP611 or Makita RT0701C.
Variable-speed version of FM 2700; electronic speed control with soft-start. 80 mm body; air-cooled; no external plumbing. ER16 collet.
Compact ER20-collet VFD spindle in 65 mm body; accepts 1/2 inch shank bits without water cooling. Quieter than trim routers. Requires VFD drive and wiring.
Higher-power trim router with both 1/4 and 1/2 inch collets included. 65 mm base fits Shapeoko and X-Carve mounts. Better for 1/2 inch shank bits vs Makita RT0701C.
12V brushless palm router; cordless option for portable CNC setups or light-duty desktop machines. 65 mm base; 1/4 inch collet only. Battery runtime limits sustained CNC use.
Premium US-made 1/2 inch upcut; high chip clearance for deep pockets. Requires ER20 or 1/2 inch collet — only fits VFD spindles or Hitachi M12VC.
1/2 inch diameter compression bit for production sheet-goods work. Clean top and bottom face. Requires ER20 collet or 1/2 inch native collet.
Carbide-tipped (brazed insert) 90° V-bit; lower cost than solid carbide; suitable for wood sign work and chamfering. Not for abrasive composites.
4-flute downcut for ultra-clean top-face finish in hardwoods. Higher feed rates than 2-flute but requires chip evacuation care in deep pockets.
Single-flute O-flute geometry for aluminum; max chip clearance prevents recutting and built-up edge. Run with lubricant mist. 1/4 inch shank.
Indexable insert body; replace worn inserts without buying new bit. Cost-effective for production. Requires ER20 or 1/2 inch collet.
45° chamfer bit (90° included) for edge-breaking and decorative chamfers. 1/4 inch shank; suits all 65 mm router mounts. Standard sign-shop edge treatment.
1/2 inch radius roundover profile for edge shaping on panels and furniture parts. 1/2 inch shank — requires ER20 or Hitachi M12VC collet.
Onsrud single-flute upcut; excellent chip evacuation for melamine and thin plywood. Premium US-made; consistent diameter tolerance.

entities.name: Plate-Edge Form Bit (ogee profile, solid carbide)

attributes.note: Ogee-profile form bit for decorative plate edges and cabinet faces. 1/2 inch shank; requires ER20 or native 1/2 inch collet. Not suitable for belt-drive machines at design feedrates.

14° dovetail (undercut) for T-slots, holding jigs, and dovetail joint pockets. 1/4 inch shank. Climb-cut recommended for cleaner walls. Requires rigid setup — not for belt-drive machines at full depth.
Large-diameter facing/spoilboard surfacing bit. 1/2 inch shank; requires ER20 collet. Run at low depth-of-cut (0.5-1 mm); wide stepover possible. Standard tool for spoilboard resurfacing.
Step clamps with T-bolt; adjustable height for stock thickness variation. Compatible with 3/4 inch T-track. Low-profile head reduces tool-path clearance requirements.
Precision adjustable fence for router table-style spoilboard setups; mounts via T-track. Good for repeat-cut production with consistent Y-axis registration.
Quick-action cam-lever clamps; tool-free clamping. Suit production runs where workpiece is swapped frequently. T-bolt slides in standard 3/4 inch T-track.
20 mm dog-hole system; clamps register workpiece against bench dogs in a 96 mm grid. Used with Parf Guide jig for drilling the dog-hole pattern.
Pre-drilled HDPE manifold plate with zone valves; drop-in replacement for MDF spoilboard vacuum tables. Zones sealed individually. Requires minimum 100 CFM pump for full hold.
Interlocking quick-mount blocks; workpiece locates on pins for repeatable zero. Quarter-turn lock. Good for small production runs with irregular stock shapes.
v3 revision with improved magnet retention and replaceable brush skirt. Mounts to Z carriage on Shapeoko; works with Makita RT0701C and DeWalt DWP611. 65 mm body fit.
OEM dust shoe for Shapeoko 4 / 5. Rigid body; mounts directly to Z-carriage. 65 mm router fit. Connects to 2.25 inch shop vac hose adapter.
Festool OEM router shroud for OF 1400 / 2200 routers; 65 mm body. Connects to Festool CT vacuum hose (27 mm). Not general-purpose — specific to Festool router body.
3D-printed adapter shoe designed to fit K40-class laser air-assist nozzles repurposed on 65 mm router body. Low-profile; adequate for light wood dust. Community-sourced STL.
PC-hosted CNC controller software with Ethernet SmoothStepper motion board. Powerful macro scripting; supports probing, tool changes, VFD spindle. Industry-standard for custom builds. Windows-only.
Centroid Acorn board + CNC12 software; PC-connected via USB. Supports auto tool changer, probing, and VFD spindle. Good upgrade path from GRBL for serious hobbyists.
Open-source real-time CNC control. Mesa 7i96S Ethernet motion card + LinuxCNC on dedicated PC. Highest flexibility and community support. Linux-only; requires RT kernel setup.

All-in-one standalone controller with 15-inch touchscreen. Runs CNC12; no external PC needed. Supports servo and stepper motors, probing, VFD spindle RS485. Aimed at retrofit and custom machine builds.
Cloud-connected CAM; includes 3D toolpath support and simulation in Pro tier. Tight integration with Shapeoko/Nomad. Free tier available with 2D-only toolpaths.
Industry-standard V-carving and 2.5D CAM. Huge post-processor library; exports to virtually any controller. No full 3D relief support — use VCarve Pro + Aspire for that.
Free for personal/hobby use with 10-active-document limit. Full 3D CAM including adaptive clearing. Cloud-based; requires internet for license check. Best-in-class toolpath quality for a free tool.
Simplified 3D CAM targeted at hobbyists; auto-toolpath generation from mesh. Less control than Fusion 360 but very low learning curve. Perpetual license.
Inexpensive 2.5D CAM with CNC controller firmware option. Very popular with low-cost GRBL machines. No 3D relief support. Also bundles its own controller firmware for Arduino-based machines.
Browser-based CAM tightly integrated with X-Carve ecosystem. Low barrier to entry; limited advanced features. Subscription required for Pro toolpath options. Good for sign and décor work.
5th-gen Shapeoko; stiffer extrusion, improved HDZ Z-axis with ballscrew Z. 65 mm mount; ships without router (Makita or Carbide Compact Router sold separately). Includes BitSetter and BitRunner v2.
Largest Onefinity; dual-rail Y for improved rigidity. ~50x50 inch cutting envelope. 80 mm spindle mount; targets semi-pro shop use. Includes Masso G3 Touch controller option.
Canadian-made; anti-backlash leadscrew drive (between belts and ballscrews in rigidity). 65 mm router mount; ships with Makita RT0701C. Open-source SLB controller compatible with gSender.
Full-sheet 4x8 production table. Rack-and-pinion; 80 mm spindle mount for 2.2 kW VFD spindle. Controller sold separately (Masso G3 or Avid AVID-CNC board). Handles full 4x8 sheet goods.

attributes.frame: aluminum extrusion, makerslide rails, extended gantry
attributes.note: Extended version of X-Carve with larger 1000x1000 mm work area. Belt-drive; 65 mm router mount. Ships with DeWalt DWP611. Easel CAM integration.

Soft-start variant of FM 1000 series; electronic speed control with slow ramp-up reduces belt/drive stress on start. German-made CNC-optimized winding. 65 mm base; direct drop-in for Shapeoko / Onefinity 65 mm mounts.

Rebranded Hitachi M12VC; identical internals. Higher power than Makita RT0701C; both 1/4 and 1/2 inch collets included. 65 mm base fits Shapeoko and X-Carve mounts. Preferred when 1/2 inch shank bits are needed without a VFD spindle.

Bare tool (no battery) Bosch 12V palm router; highest RPM in class at 32,000. Lightweight; suited for foam, thin wood, and soft plastics on desktop CNCs. 65 mm base. Sustained CNC use limited by battery runtime.

entities.name: ER25 1.5 kW 80 mm Air-Cooled VFD Spindle

attributes.note: ER25 collet extends shank capacity to 5/8 inch — accepts 1/2 inch shank bits comfortably. 80 mm body for Onefinity / Avid mounts. Air-cooled; no external water system. Requires VFD drive and 220 V wiring.

entities.name: ER32 4 kW 80 mm Water-Cooled VFD Spindle

attributes.note: High-power industrial-class spindle; ER32 collet accepts up to 3/4 inch shank. Lower max RPM than 2.2 kW models — suited for large-diameter bits and heavy cuts in hardwood or aluminum. Requires 3-phase VFD or single-phase 220 V VFD rated ≥4 kW.
Whiteside RU2100: US-made solid carbide upcut spiral. Consistent grind tolerance; popular benchmark for sign and cabinet work. Ships in individual packaging with specs card.
Whiteside RD2200: compression spiral with downcut top flute and upcut bottom flute. Clean top and bottom simultaneously. US-made; tighter runout spec than generic compression bits.
Amana 51404 ZrN-coated single O-flute for aluminum; polished flute reduces built-up edge. Run with lubricant mist (WD-40 or Tap Magic). Low chip recutting risk. 1/4 inch shank.
Amana 45776: 60° solid carbide V-bit for tight serif letterforms and thin V-carve lines. 1/4 inch shank; universal mount. Sharp tip for fine detail inlay clearance.
Amana 46479: 3D finishing ballnose; consistent radius ground to ±0.001 inch. Standard 3D relief finishing pass at 10–15% stepover in wood and MDF.

Amana 51425: longer LOC variant of the aluminum O-flute series. ZrN coating for extended tool life in aluminum. Extra flute length allows deeper single-pass cuts. Requires VFD spindle or router with ER collet.

ZrN (Zirconium Nitride) coating improves wear resistance vs uncoated carbide; suited for hardwood and HDPE detail passes. 4-flute reduces chip load per tooth; requires good chip evacuation in deep pockets.
Spektra is Amana's multi-layer nano-coating line; improved edge retention vs uncoated. Single O-flute geometry for excellent chip evacuation. Best-in-class for plywood and acrylic in one tool.

Budget 14° dovetail undercut bit; 1/4 inch shank. Suited for T-slot spoilboard cutting and dovetail joint pockets. Lower runout spec than Amana — acceptable for wood joinery, not recommended for tight-tolerance jig making.

entities.name: Bits and Bits 1/8" Micro Endmill 0.0625" (solid carbide)

attributes.note: Bits and Bits micro endmill with 1/8 inch shank (necked to 1/16 inch cutting diameter). US-sourced; tighter diameter tolerance than generic imports. Requires ballscrew machine; feed ≤500 mm/min.
Budget spoilboard surfacing / slab flattening bit; 1/2 inch cutting diameter, 1/4 inch shank. Lower cost than 1.5 inch surfacing bits; requires more passes but fits any router with 1/4 inch collet.

Donek D2: improved blade holder with replaceable Dexter blade cartridge. Tangential drag knife; spindle must not rotate during use. Compatible with any 1/4 inch collet. Tangential knife offset required in CAM.
Single-flute long LOC bit optimized for EPS/XPS foam sign blanks and prop making. High RPM + low feed recommended to prevent foam smear. No coolant needed.
Extended LOC (1.75 inch) for full-depth single-pass cuts in 3/4 inch plywood. Rated for plywood gummy glue lines. Extra reach for deep pocket work in cabinet panels.
60° solid carbide V-bit suitable for engraving soft metals (brass, aluminum) at low depth-of-cut. Ground to sharp tip for hairline engraving. Run with cutting oil on metals.
Diamond-pattern or DLC-coated endmill for abrasive composite materials. Wear dust mask and use dust collection — fiberglass dust is hazardous. Low DOC per pass to minimize heat.

Single O-flute geometry for thermoplastics; maximizes chip clearance and prevents melt/reweld. Run at moderate RPM (18,000–22,000) to stay below plastic melting threshold. Suitable for Lexan and cast acrylic sheet.

Ceramic-tipped endmill; extremely hard edge retains sharpness in abrasive or hard materials. Brittle — not for interrupted cuts or plunge entry. Requires high RPM and flood/mist cooling. Not suited for wood or plastics.

entities.name: Kreg CNC Routing Table Track System (wood spoilboard)

attributes.note: Kreg-branded aluminum T-track sections for embedding in wood/MDF spoilboards. Accepts Kreg's quick-release clamps and F-clamps. Easy router or dado installation into a custom spoilboard.
Parallel-jaw bar clamp; used off-machine for glue-up and panel lamination before CNC fixturing. Not a T-slot clamp. Best paired with spoilboard dog-hole system for edge clamping irregular stock.
Two-piece wedge clamp: base seats in T-slot, wedge drives against workpiece edge. Ultra-low profile (clears most toolpaths). Sold in 10-pack; sufficient for a 4x4 spoilboard perimeter fixture.
Quarter-turn cam-action T-bolt clamps; tool-free swap. Low-profile head clears most router toolpaths. Best for production runs with frequent stock swaps. Fits standard 3/4 inch T-track.

entities.name: MFT Vise Dogs + Clamping Kit (20 mm)

attributes.note: 20 mm dog system with vise-style clamping dogs; workpiece registers against fixed dog and is clamped by threaded vise dog. Ideal for repeat production where XY zero must be repeatable. Requires Parf Guide jig to drill 96 mm grid dog-hole pattern.

Sweepy 2.0: redesigned Z-height adjustment and magnetic brush skirt swap. Mounts directly to Shapeoko Z-carriage without tools. Fits 65 mm routers (Makita RT0701C, DeWalt DWP611). 2.25 inch hose port.
Pwncnc 65 mm dust boot; polycarbonate body with magnetic brush skirt. Adjustable Z-height clearance. Fits Shapeoko, X-Carve, LongMill (65 mm mount). Compatible with Makita RT0701C and DeWalt DWP611.
OEM Onefinity dust shoe for 80 mm spindle body (VFD spindle or Mafell FM 2700). Mk3 revision adds improved brush retention and 2.25 inch hose port. Does not fit 65 mm routers without adapter.

entities.name: Mafell Brush Attachment for FM series

attributes.note: OEM Mafell dust extraction brush collar; clips onto FM 1000 / FM 2700 router body. 27 mm hose port — requires Festool CT dust extractor or adapter to standard shop vac hose. Not compatible with generic 65 mm dust shoes.

Centroid Acorn V2: updated Acorn board with improved USB isolation and onboard relay for spindle. Runs CNC12 software on connected Windows PC. Supports ATC, probing, VFD spindle via RS485. Popular retrofit controller for router tables and custom builds.

Mesa 7i97T: Ethernet motion card with onboard analog spindle output (0–10 V) for direct VFD speed control. LinuxCNC on RT-patched Linux PC. Open-source; highest flexibility. Requires RT kernel and HAL configuration — significant setup effort.

Current Mach4 + ESS pairing; ESS handles real-time pulse generation over Ethernet, offloading timing from PC. Windows-only. Supports VFD spindle via Modbus, probing, rigid tapping. Industry-standard for semi-pro custom router builds.

2025 firmware revision adds improved VFD RS485 spindle control and expanded probe input support. Linux-based (RPi); web UI + optional touchscreen. Used in Onefinity machines. Not GRBL — uses own G-code dialect; most CAM post-processors support it.

Aspire 13: full 3D relief carving, V-carving, and nesting. Superset of VCarve Pro with sculpting, texture, and two-sided machining. Perpetual license; huge post-processor library. Industry-standard for sign shops and 3D carving.
Carveco Maker: subscription-based 3D relief CAM. ArtCAM heritage; good for artistic 3D carving. Lower entry cost than Aspire. Monthly or annual subscription. Windows-only.
Simple 2.5D DXF/SVG-to-G-code workflow; minimal learning curve. Perpetual license. Suited for sign work, pockets, and profiles. No 3D relief capability. Cross-platform (Windows + macOS).

FreeCAD Path (formerly Path workbench): open-source parametric CAD + CAM. Full 3D toolpath support including adaptive clearing. Cross-platform. Steeper learning curve than commercial tools; active community. No subscription cost.

Pre-cut 4x4 MDF sheet for use as a CNC spoilboard. Must be surfaced flat with the machine's own spindle before use to establish Z-plane coplanarity. Replace when surface material loss exceeds 3–4 mm. Low-cost consumable.

Pre-drilled HDPE manifold plate with zone isolation valves for 4x4 table. Zones allow partial-sheet hold without covering full surface. Requires ≥100 CFM vacuum pump for reliable hold on full-size sheet stock.
MDF spoilboard with pre-routed slots and aluminum T-track sections embedded for hold-down clamp flexibility. Allows T-bolt clamps and step clamps to slide freely anywhere on the surface.

Adjustable levelling feet and precision shims for CNC machine base plates. Critical for machines on non-flat benchtops; ensures gantry tramming and Z-axis perpendicularity. Compatible with most router table and CNC stand leg configurations.

Rubber-isolated levelling feet for CNC machine bases. Reduce transmitted vibration to bench and floor; reduce noise by 3–6 dB in typical shop environments. Load rating: 50–150 kg per foot depending on variant. Improves surface finish at high feed rates.

constraints.description: A router or VFD spindle can only be mounted on a machine whose spindle_mount_diameter_mm matches the spindle's base_diameter_mm. 65 mm mounts (Shapeoko, X-Carve, BobsCNC, Genmitsu) accept 65 mm body routers/spindles. 80 mm mounts (Onefinity, Avid) accept 80 mm body spindles. 52 mm mounts (Carvera, PROVerXL 4030) use integrated spindles only.

A bit's shank_diameter_inch must be <= the collet's maximum capacity. Makita RT0701C and DeWalt DWP611 accept only 1/4 inch shank natively. 1/8 inch shank bits require a 1/4-to-1/8 inch precision reducer sleeve (not recommended for production) OR an ER11/ER16 collet set on a VFD spindle. Never force a 1/4 inch shank into an ER11 (max 7 mm ≈ 0.276 inch — fits, but tight; verify collet rating).

ER11 collet accepts 1/16 to 1/4 inch shanks. ER16 collet accepts 1/16 to 3/8 inch shanks. ER20 collet accepts 1/16 to 1/2 inch shanks. Bit shank_diameter_inch must fall within the spindle's ER collet range when er_collet_compatible is true.

A dust shoe's fits_router_diameter_mm must match the mounted spindle's base_diameter_mm. 65 mm dust shoes (SuckIt, Rockler, generic) only fit 65 mm body routers. They do NOT fit 80 mm VFD spindles without an 80-to-65 mm adapter ring.

The drag knife bit must be used with spindle not rotating (S0 in G-code or relay off). Using a drag knife in a spinning collet will destroy the tool and workpiece. CAM must output tangential knife offsets, not a rotating toolpath.

The 1/16 inch micro endmill is unsuitable for belt-drive machines (Shapeoko 4, BobsCNC E4, X-Carve Pro) at feeds above 500 mm/min due to deflection amplified by belt compliance. Ballscrew machines (Onefinity, Avid, Carvera, Genmitsu PROVerXL) are required for micro endmill work.

VFD spindles (1.5 kW air-cooled, 2.2 kW water-cooled) require a separate VFD drive unit wired between the controller and spindle. Machines with integrated spindles (Carvera, Genmitsu PROVerXL) cannot accept VFD spindle upgrades without significant electrical modification. Masso G3 and Buildbotics both support VFD RS485/Modbus spindle control.

The DIY vacuum table fixture only holds stock that fully covers at least one vacuum zone and forms an airtight perimeter seal. Stock with large through-holes or irregular perimeters will not hold. Works best with sheet stock 300x300 mm or larger with flat underside.
